tactical_shape: Played with creativity but struggled to find spaces against a structured low block
Used wide/attacking pressure to create chances, but the punditry mainly describes persistence rather than a fixed formation
standout_players: Daniel Muñoz: scored the winner, his second goal in as many games
Lionel Mpasi (opposition goalkeeper) repeatedly denied Colombia, especially Jhon Arias, James Rodríguez and Luis Díaz
James Rodríguez: had an early shot saved
Jhon Arias: had chances saved and later missed the rebound wide
Luis Díaz: had two goals ruled out for offside and was repeatedly denied
Johan Mojica: tested Mpasi with a long-range effort
home_read.momentum: Qualified for the World Cup knockouts with a second straight win, but the punditry says they must become more efficient against compact low blocks and still have work to do to top the group.

tactical_shape: Sat in a low block / retreating behind the barricades
Switched to a tightened-up midfield after early pressure
Used a back five for the final three-quarters of the game
Midfield three protected the defence, with Yoane Wissa linking to a lone centre-forward
standout_players: Lionel Mpasi: 'excellent today' and made an outrageous string of saves
Sébastien Desabre: credited for tweaks that tightened the midfield
Noah Sadiki: introduced at half-time; his engine helped prevent the side running out of legs
Edo Kayembe: had the first shot of the game, just wide
Nathanaël Mbuku: late long-range drive forced Camilo Vargas into a save
away_read.momentum: Defiant and hard to break down, but eventually beaten; still alive in the group and likely need to take more risks in the final game.
